task_completion.description: Verify the three top-level pages are created and published.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the WordPress 'Pages' list. 'About the Department', 'Academic Programs', and 'Research & Scholarship' should be listed without a dash prefix. They must not have a 'Draft' or 'Pending' label next to them, indicating they are published.

task_completion.description: Verify the children of 'About the Department' are created, published, and correctly parented.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Pages' list, look for 'Mission Statement', 'Faculty Directory', and 'Contact Information'. They should appear directly under 'About the Department' with a dash prefix (e.g., '— Mission Statement') indicating they are child pages. They must not have a 'Draft' label.

task_completion.description: Verify the children of 'Academic Programs' are created, published, and correctly parented.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Pages' list, look for 'Undergraduate Studies', 'Graduate Studies', and 'Course Catalog'. They should appear directly under 'Academic Programs' with a dash prefix indicating they are child pages. They must not have a 'Draft' label.

task_completion.description: Verify the children of 'Research & Scholarship' are created, published, and correctly parented.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Pages' list, look for 'Current Projects', 'Publications', and 'Funding Opportunities'. They should appear directly under 'Research & Scholarship' with a dash prefix indicating they are child pages. They must not have a 'Draft' label.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'Order' attribute is correctly set for the pages according to the specifications.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at screenshots of the WordPress page editor (specifically the 'Page Attributes' panel on the right side) for the various pages. Verify the 'Order' field is set to 1, 2, or 3 as specified in the task description for both top-level and child pages.

task_completion.description: Verify the specific required text content has been added to each of the 12 pages.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Examine the screenshots showing the WordPress block editor or page previews for each page. Verify the specific text snippets (e.g., 'cutting-edge research', 'Dr. Sarah Chen', 'DH 101', 'National Endowment for the Humanities') are present in the main content area of their respective pages.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the WordPress web interface to create the pages.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for interaction with a web browser showing the WordPress admin dashboard (wp-admin). The agent should not be using a terminal to run WP-CLI commands or direct database SQL queries.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual WordPress application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the standard WordPress UI elements like the left-hand admin menu, the top admin bar, and the Gutenberg block editor, rather than a text editor or a mocked interface.

integrity.description: Verify the agent manually navigated and entered the data rather than using an automated script shortcut.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for a sequence of screenshots showing the 'Add New Page' screen being opened, titles being typed, content being added, and the 'Publish' button being clicked multiple times.
